reasoning_model_1: Model 1 demonstrates perfect content accuracy, adhering strictly to the provided script without any additions or omissions. Regarding the core paralinguistic feature (Speed/语速), the model executes the instruction flawlessly. The segment from 00:00 to 00:02 ('快！冲上去！他没血了！') is delivered with high urgency and a fast tempo, effectively conveying the excitement of the combat scenario. There is a distinct and natural transition at 00:03 on the word '等等' (Wait), serving as a brake. From 00:04 to 00:06 ('这个宝箱里到底有什么？'), the speech rate shifts to be extremely slow and deliberate, matching the 'curiosity' and 'sudden deceleration' requested. The contrast between the two sections is sharp and effective.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails primarily on Content Accuracy. At the very beginning (00:00), the model reads out part of the prompt instruction '快速开始' (Start fast) as part of the speech, resulting in the spoken text: '快速开始，快冲上去...'. This is a hallucination/instruction leakage error. Regarding the core feature, the model does attempt the speed change; the first half is relatively fast, and it slows down for the second half ('等等，这个宝箱...'). However, the initial speed is somewhat muddled by the extra phrase, and the deceleration, while present, is less dramatic and effective than Model 1 due to the distraction of the content error.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed all instructions, including content and paralinguistic adjustments, perfectly. The transition from fast to slow was distinct and dramatic, enhancing the storytelling. Model 2 committed a significant error by reading the instruction '快速开始' aloud, which violates the content accuracy criterion. While Model 2 did attempt the speed change, the inclusion of non-script text severely degrades the quality and usability of the generation.
